name: Bengal Subah

colors.symbolism: the scimitar hilt and the three barrels (gold)

description: A white field bearing a black scimitar with a gold hilt and three gold barrels.
history: Bengal Subah was the largest province (subah) of the Mughal Empire. After 1717 it became the de facto independent domain of the hereditary Nawabs of Bengal under nominal Mughal suzerainty, until the British East India Company gained control following Plassey (1757). The reconstruction shown here is the nautical (merchant) flag attributed to Bengal in Flags of all nations (1862).
